tasks.name: Initialize Tauri 2 + React project scaffold
tasks.description: Scaffold a runnable Tauri 2 + React 18 + Vite project matching the directory layout in architecture §3.2.
output: Runnable `npm run dev` with a Tauri window showing a blank React app
src-tauri/ and src/ directories matching §3.2 layout
acceptance_criteria: `npm run dev` opens a native window without errors
src-tauri/src/main.rs registers zero commands (placeholder only)
src/App.tsx renders a placeholder div
ESLint and Rust clippy both pass cleanly

tasks.name: Implement config.rs — .aiworkspace JSON read/write
tasks.description: Typed read/write functions for every JSON file under ~/.aiworkspace/ and <project>/.aiworkspace/. The only module in the codebase that performs file I/O.
output: src-tauri/src/config.rs with typed read/write functions for projects.json, workspace.json, environments.json, collections.json, secrets.json
acceptance_criteria: Each file type has a dedicated read/write function returning a typed Rust struct
Missing files return a typed default (not an error)
Secrets files are created with 0600 permissions
No other Rust module does direct file I/O — all goes through config.rs

tasks.name: Implement pty_manager.rs — terminal process lifecycle
tasks.description: PTY lifecycle management using portable-pty and tmux for session persistence.
output: src-tauri/src/commands/terminal.rs exposing create_terminal, write_terminal, resize_terminal, close_terminal Tauri commands
acceptance_criteria: create_terminal spawns a tmux session and returns a terminal ID
write_terminal writes bytes to the PTY stdin
resize_terminal sends SIGWINCH with new cols/rows
Terminal session survives app restart (tmux keeps it alive)
Closing the app does not kill the tmux session

tasks.name: Create projects.ts Zustand store
tasks.description: Zustand store for the project list and active project ID. Source of truth for which projects exist.
acceptance_criteria: Actions: loadProjects, addProject, removeProject, setActiveProject
All mutations call Tauri commands/projects.rs — store never writes files directly
activeProjectId is the single source of truth for which project is selected
No workspace state in this store (that belongs in workspace.ts)

tasks.name: Create workspace.ts Zustand store
tasks.description: Per-project panel state store, scoped to the active project. Resets and reloads on project switch.
output: src/store/workspace.ts scoped to the active project
acceptance_criteria: State resets and reloads from disk whenever activeProjectId changes in projects.ts
Actions: setActivePanel, setBrowserUrl, setOpenFiles
All mutations persist to workspace.json via Tauri — no direct file writes from React
projects.ts and workspace.ts share no state fields

tasks.name: Build ProjectList sidebar component
tasks.description: Left sidebar showing all projects with add (+) button, active highlight, and sub-200ms switching.
output: src/components/ProjectList/ — left sidebar listing projects with add (+) button
acceptance_criteria: Clicking a project calls switchProject and completes in <200ms (§5.1)
(+) button triggers native Tauri folder picker dialog
Active project is visually highlighted
Component reads only from projects.ts store — no direct Tauri calls
Switching saves current workspace.json then loads new one before re-rendering

tasks.name: Build TerminalPanel component with xterm.js
tasks.description: xterm.js terminal panel connected to PTY. Hosts Claude Code.
output: src/components/panels/TerminalPanel/ rendering a full PTY terminal
acceptance_criteria: xterm.js renders in the panel and connects to a PTY via create_terminal on mount
Keyboard input is forwarded to PTY via write_terminal
Terminal resizes correctly when panel resizes (SIGWINCH)
Terminal state (tmux session name) is stored in workspace.json so it reconnects on restart
Claude Code can be launched inside the terminal and operates normally

tasks.name: Implement sendToClaudeCode.ts — core PTY integration
tasks.description: The reason the app exists. Pipes runtime context into the active Claude Code session via MCP (primary) or PTY write (fallback).
acceptance_criteria: Primary transport: writes to .claude/inbox/NNNN.md and emits MCP notification when MCP session is active
Fallback transport: writes formatted string to PTY via write_terminal with '——— AIWorkspace context ———' marker
UI badge shows which transport was used (MCP or PTY)
After sending, terminal panel scrolls into view
Send button flashes green for 1 second after send
This is the ONLY file in the codebase that calls write_terminal

tasks.name: Implement MCP server — mcp_server.rs + mcp_tools.rs
tasks.description: Local MCP server (Tauri sidecar) exposing all 20 tools from §8.2 over stdio.
acceptance_criteria: MCP server starts as Tauri sidecar on app launch
All 20 tools from §8.2 are defined with correct input/output schemas
Tool calls are dispatched to mcp_bridge.rs — mcp_tools.rs contains no business logic
`aiworkspace-mcp --list-tools` prints all tools in MCP format
Server communicates over stdio only (no network port)

tasks.name: Implement mcp_bridge.rs + mcpBridge.ts — IPC bridge
tasks.description: IPC bridge between MCP sidecar and main Tauri window. Enforces destructive query gate and secret redaction.
acceptance_criteria: mcp_bridge.rs forwards MCP tool calls to the main Tauri window via Tauri events
mcpBridge.ts listens for events and executes them against panel state
Destructive SQL gate (is_destructive() from §10.4) is enforced in mcp_bridge.rs before any db_query executes
Secrets are stripped from all MCP tool responses before returning to Claude Code
Browser panel WebView has IPC disabled — cannot reach mcp_bridge.rs (§13.1)

tasks.name: Write .claude/mcp.json on project open
tasks.description: Auto-configuration of Claude Code's MCP connection when a project opens. Must respect existing config (§12 R5).
output: Auto-written .claude/mcp.json in project root on every project open
acceptance_criteria: File is written with correct aiworkspace-mcp command and --project path on project open
If .claude/mcp.json already exists, prompt the developer before overwriting (§12 R5)
`.claude/mcp.json` is added to project .gitignore automatically
A global 'never auto-configure MCP' toggle in settings suppresses this behavior

tasks.name: Build BrowserPanel with console capture
tasks.description: Embedded WebView with JS error and network failure capture. Each error has a Send to Claude Code button.
input: MCP tools: browser_navigate, browser_get_console_logs, browser_screenshot, browser_get_dom, browser_click, browser_fill
output: src/components/panels/BrowserPanel/ — embedded WebView + console log sidebar + Send to Claude Code button per error
acceptance_criteria: WebView navigates to URLs stored in workspace.json (per-project browser URL)
JS errors and network failures appear in the console sidebar
Each console entry has a 'Send to Claude Code' button that calls sendToClaudeCode.ts
MCP tools browser_navigate, browser_screenshot, browser_get_console_logs work end-to-end
WebView runs in a separate partition with Tauri IPC disabled

tasks.name: Build HttpPanel with shared executor and request log
tasks.description: Postman-equivalent HTTP client. Shared executor used by both Send button and MCP — one code path, one log.
acceptance_criteria: httpExecutor.ts is the single HTTP execution path — used by both Send button and MCP bridge
Request log shows [You] or [Claude Code] source label per entry (§9.2)
Clicking a log entry loads it into the request editor
Collections stored in .aiworkspace/http/collections.json (git-tracked)
Request log is session-only (not persisted to disk)
Environment variable interpolation ({{base_url}}) resolves correctly at fire time

tasks.name: Build DbPanel with query log and destructive gate UI
tasks.description: Unified database + cache panel (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis). Destructive MCP queries surface a confirmation dialog.
input: MCP tools: db_query, db_list_connections, db_list_tables, db_describe_table, cache_get, cache_set, cache_keys, cache_flush
output: src/components/panels/DbPanel/ — query editor, schema browser, query log, confirmation dialog
acceptance_criteria: Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis connections
Query log shows [You], [Claude Code], [BLOCKED] source labels (§10.3)
Destructive queries from MCP trigger a developer confirmation dialog — Claude Code waits for response
Developer-initiated queries in the editor are NOT gated
Connections stored in .aiworkspace/connections.json (gitignored)
cache_flush always shows confirmation dialog regardless of pattern

tasks.name: Implement environment + secrets system
tasks.description: Named environment sets with variable interpolation. Four-tier resolution. Secrets never leave the main process.
acceptance_criteria: Resolution order matches §11.2: runtime tokens → env plain values → project secrets → global secrets
{{variable}} references in requests, queries, and connection strings resolve at fire time
Unresolved variables shown in red in the UI
MCP env_get_variables returns plain values only — secrets redacted as {{SECRET}}
Token capture rule on HTTP request stores result in memory (Zustand) with optional TTL
environments.json is git-tracked; secrets.json is always gitignored

tasks.name: Build EditorPanel with Monaco
tasks.description: Monaco-based file viewer/editor for reading files Claude Code just edited. Not a full IDE replacement.
acceptance_criteria: Opens files listed in workspace.json openFiles on panel mount
File reads go through commands/fs.rs — no direct filesystem access from React
workspace_get_context MCP tool returns active file, open files, and project path
Read-only by default; edits require explicit opt-in (no accidental overwrites)
Language mode auto-detected from file extension
